commit.message:
fix: update build-appimage.sh for Ubuntu 24.04 local builds

- Build SDL3 from deps/SDL locally rather than expecting a system install
  at /usr/local/lib; cmake build is skipped if already built
- Compile a glibc version shim (LD_PRELOAD) to bypass linuxdeployqt's
  refusal to run on glibc > 2.35 (Ubuntu 22.04 Jammy requirement)
- Extract appimagetool from the linuxdeployqt AppImage and run
  linuxdeployqt without -appimage so bundled libs can be stripped before
  the final AppImage is packaged
- Strip libQt6*.so* from the deploy folder (use system Qt on target to
  avoid version mismatch)
- Strip libssl.so.3 and libcrypto.so.3 from the deploy folder (use system
  OpenSSL on target to avoid version mismatch with system libcurl)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add note about password not showing when typing sudo

Add notion-agents MCP server CLI with agent trigger support

commit.message:
docs: Full 1:1 endpoint audit — 548/985 consumed (56%)

Previous commit used decorator counts and URL-pattern classification.
This commit resolves every backend route to its full path (APIRouter
prefix + @router path), then matches each 1:1 against every frontend
apiClient.* call with normalized template literals.

Results: 985 unique backend endpoints (997 raw, 12 cross-file
duplicates), 543 exact 1:1 matches + 5 runtime-resolved guardian
paths = 548 consumed (56%). 14 phantom consumers identified and
documented. 10 consumed + 10 orphaned spot-checked against source.

Key domain findings: Reporting 95%, Approvals 80%, but Guardian
System only 18% (120 endpoints, 22 consumed) and Ownership 13%
(owner sub-resource CRUD all orphaned).

commit.message:
Make FCollection's set field transient to skip redundant serialization (#9991)

The set duplicates all list elements during Java serialization. Since
the list is the source of truth, rebuild the set from it in readObject()
instead of serializing it. Also fixes HashMap bucket corruption on
deserialization of circular object graphs.

commit.message:
Fix single-workflow AI generation and harden all AI endpoints

- Fix generate-ai-workflow endpoint: fetch workflow data from DB instead of
  relying on empty client params (root cause of silent generation failure)
- Increase max_tokens from 4096 to 8192 across all AI generation endpoints
  to prevent response truncation on complex workflows
- Add parseAIJsonArray helper with markdown code block stripping and
  truncated JSON repair (bracket counting + trailing comma fix)
- Increase Vercel function timeout from 60s to 300s for AI generation
- Increase client-side AI request timeout from 60s to 180s
- Fix SPA routing: use routes with filesystem handler instead of rewrites

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

admin: establish typography scale — replace 50+ hardcoded font values with variables

Define a 9-step type scale and 4 weight variables in :root:

  --text-xs   0.75rem   badges, captions, table headers
  --text-sm   0.85rem   secondary labels, helper text
  --text-base 0.92rem   body text, table cells
  --text-md   1rem      standard UI, buttons
  --text-lg   1.125rem  minor headings, h3
  --text-xl   1.25rem   card headings, h2
  --text-2xl  1.5rem    page/panel headings, h1
  --text-3xl  2rem      KPI values, metric numbers
  --text-4xl  3rem      hero/display numbers

  --font-normal 400  --font-medium 500
  --font-semibold 600  --font-bold 700

Set semantic defaults so elements work without custom classes:
  h1 → text-2xl/bold   h2 → text-xl/semibold   h3 → text-lg/semibold
  p → text-base   small → text-xs/muted   td → text-sm
  th → text-xs   button → text-base/semibold

Replace all 196 hardcoded font-size declarations and 100 font-weight
declarations across the 6056-line stylesheet with the new variables.
Zero hardcoded font values remain (clamp() responsive values kept as-is).

Add utility classes: .text-{xs…4xl}, .font-{normal…bold},
.text-muted, .text-secondary for cases where semantic HTML isn't enough.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Warm-up state machine on startup by replaying recent session bars

- Add StockScanner.warm_up(): replays last N regular-session bars per
  symbol without emitting alerts, so the state machine reaches the
  correct current state (TRENDING/PULLBACK) on startup.
- Extract _step() from process() to share state-transition logic between
  normal scan ticks and warm-up replay.
- Replace initial _scan_cycle() call in ScannerThread.run() with
  _warm_up_cycle() which calls warm_up() instead of process().

commit.message:
prompt: winter_1 and fall_2 season instruction rewrites

winter_1 — greeting first: one warm sentence (door opening), then one
small true observation from the territory their word points toward —
not the encyclopedia entry, something actually noticed. Two sentences
total maximum, then stop.

fall_2 — direction then question: name one person/trail/honest guess,
then ask directly "would you like to speak with them?" — a question
that requires an answer, not a floating suggestion. Door open, walk
ends by ending. Updated in both STROLL_GARDENER_BASE prose and the
buildStrollSeasonalInstruction instructions object.

Fix frantic panel toggling and Fathom matching issues

- Increase polling interval from 2s to 3s
- Remove hardcoded isFathomAlreadyInMeeting check — now just matches

- Generalize name extraction from participant containers

commit.message:
Add TPC-DS plan translation coverage test (#383)

* Add TPC-DS plan translation coverage test

Registers a `sirius_plan_check(query)` table function that runs the full
DuckDB-to-Sirius plan translation pipeline (parse, plan, optimize,
column binding, sirius_physical_plan_generator::create_plan) without
GPU execution, then reports success/failure per query.

Uses DuckDB's tpcds extension for schema and queries. Groups failures
by error type for easy identification of missing operator support.

Run: ./build/release/extension/sirius/test/cpp/sirius_unittest "[tpcds]" -s

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add TPC-DS plan translation test for legacy GPU planner

Add a second test case that exercises GPUPhysicalPlanGenerator::CreatePlan
alongside the existing sirius_physical_plan_generator::create_plan test,
allowing side-by-side comparison of plan translation coverage between the
legacy and new planners. Extract common test loop into a shared helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Avoid OOM in legacy GPU plan translation test

GPU physical operators allocate CUDA memory in their constructors
(e.g. GPUPhysicalTableScan calls GPUBufferManager::customCudaHostAlloc),
which fails without an initialized GPU buffer. Replace the direct
GPUPhysicalPlanGenerator::CreatePlan call with a recursive walk of the
logical plan tree that checks operator support against the legacy
planner's dispatch table, avoiding GPU operator construction entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
perf(core,server): SQLite tuning — busy_timeout, temp_store, WAL checkpoint, optimize, indexes

Increase busy_timeout from 5s to 30s to prevent "database is locked" during
scans. Add temp_store=memory for faster temp tables. Run WAL checkpoint and
PRAGMA optimize after each pipeline cycle. Add indexes on tracks(file_path),
albums(year), and albums(rating).

Add force update button to taskbar with real-time progress UI

Add a force update button to the taskbar that allows users to upgrade
to the latest version with real-time progress feedback.

Features:
- Update button in taskbar (refresh icon)
- Version check modal showing current and latest versions
- Real-time WebSocket progress updates
- Progress bar with percentage
- Color-coded log messages (info/success/error/progress)
- Automatic service restart detection
- Auto page reload after successful update

UI Components:
- Taskbar button: Refresh icon next to upload button
- Update modal: Version info, progress bar, logs, action buttons
- Progress states: Checking → Updating → Success/Error
- Log colors: Blue (info), Green (success), Red (error), Sky (progress)

User Flow:
1. Click update button in taskbar
2. Modal shows version comparison
3. Confirm update
4. Real-time progress with logs
5. Wait for service restart
6. Auto reload page

Location:
- Button: Taskbar (after upload button)
- Modal: Center overlay with dark theme
- Logs: Scrollable terminal-style display

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: SnapForge — privacy-first macOS screenshot tool with on-device AI

A complete macOS menu bar app built with Swift 6, SwiftUI 6, and SwiftPM (no Xcode required).

Capture modes: screenshot, scrolling capture, screen recording (H.265/H.264/ProRes),
GIF recording, OCR text extraction, and pin-to-desktop.

Core features:
- On-device AI via Core ML & MLX (cloud providers opt-in)
- Searchable library with SQLite FTS5 full-text search
- Floating action bar with per-type memory
- Command palette with fuzzy matching
- Localhost REST API for automation
- Siri Shortcuts + URL scheme integration
- AES-GCM encrypted sharing with Keychain storage
- Zero telemetry, fully offline by default

Architecture: actor-based concurrency, @Observable state,
protocol-based AI providers, dual persistence (SwiftData + FTS5).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: GPU-accelerated encoding via h264_nvenc with CPU fallback

Auto-detects NVENC at startup with a 1-frame test encode. When available,
switches all re-encoding endpoints (compress, resize, speed, watermark, cartoon)
from libx264 to h264_nvenc (VBR mode, p4/p6 preset) for 10-50x faster processing.
Falls back to libx264 transparently if NVENC is unavailable.

commit.message:
admin: redesign KpiCard with explicit 4-area grid layout

Replace the implicit icon+content column layout with a named 4-zone grid:

  ┌──────────┬──────────────────┐
  │ topLeft  │ topRight         │
  │ (icon)   │ (label)          │
  ├──────────┼──────────────────┤
  │ bottomLeft│ bottomRight     │
  │ (empty)  │ (value + extra)  │
  └──────────┴──────────────────┘

Default slot mapping from convenience props keeps existing visual output:
  icon → topLeft, label → topRight, value+children → bottomRight.
Any slot can be overridden via topLeft/topRight/bottomLeft/bottomRight props,
making it easy to place custom content in any quadrant on future pages.

Add .kpi-card-grid modifier class and .kpi-card-{tl,tr,bl,br} CSS rules
to styles.css alongside the existing .buyer-v2-kpi styles.

commit.message:
Fix Docker release: copy runtime.exs into build context

The Dockerfile was missing COPY for config/runtime.exs, so the release
had no server:true, http: or secret_key_base config — the HTTP server
never started and health checks failed.

Also fix force_ssl exclude config (was a sibling key, now nested).

Verified locally: container starts, /up returns 200, health goes healthy.

commit.message:
fix: correct padEnd/escapeBlessed ordering in dashboard TUI (#45)

padEnd must run on the raw string before escapeBlessed(), since escape
inflates the string length ({→{open}). Fixes column alignment in
formatBranch() and formatConflict(). Header box already has tags:false
so no escaping needed there.

Resolves the fixes identified in PR #45 review.
